steps.title: Set up the volume formula for a square prism
steps.content:
A right square prism has volume equal to base area times height. The base is a square with side length $s$, so the base area is $s^2$. Substituting the given values $V = 2016$ and $h = 14$:
$$2016 = s^2 \cdot 14$$

steps.title: Solve for s² by dividing both sides by the height
Take the square root to find the edge length
steps.content:
Since $s$ represents a length and must be positive:
$$s = \sqrt{144} = 12$$
The edge length of the base is 12 units.
